$comment: ADOS Reference Model Bank — Verifiability Class records (eval facet E; docs/REDESIGN-PROPOSAL.md §4.5). Tags whether an item even HAS a checkable answer, which protects the IQ metric: 'objective' and 'rubric' items count toward IQ (rubric weighted by etalon agreement κ); 'subjective' items have no defensible single key and are reported as a preference rate (pref%), EXCLUDED from the IQ/accuracy number. This is the single most important guard against pretending a creative task has a 'correct' archetype the way an extraction task does. Validates against schema/ontology_banks.schema.json.
materiality_test: If a competent independent judge could not agree on a single correct answer, the item is not 'objective'; if no defensible key exists at all, it is 'subjective'.
drift_guards: 'objective' requires a genuinely checkable answer or it is 'rubric'.
'subjective' items NEVER enter the IQ/accuracy number (reported as pref% apart).
Verifiability is per-item: a cognitive type's default can be overridden by the item's actual checkability.

verifiability_classes.label: Objective
verifiability_classes.scoring: Deterministic check or high-agreement consensus against a single correct answer.

verifiability_classes.label: Rubric-gradeable
verifiability_classes.scoring: Graded against explicit criteria; etalon = rubric-scored consensus, weighted by inter-judge agreement (κ).

verifiability_classes.label: Subjective
verifiability_classes.scoring: Taste/style preference; no defensible single key. Reported as a preference win-rate.
